title: planchonella ericiflora
intro: Planchonella ericiflora, also known as the White Apple or Boreo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Planchonella ericiflora are attributed to its leaves and fruits, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Planchonella ericiflora include reducing fever, treating wounds and infections, and aiding in the relief of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Planchonella ericiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the leaves and fruits, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
